response.justification: While the candidate brings valuable geospatial domain expertise and a strong testing-first mindset, the critical gap in modern C++ proficiency makes this a poor fit for a role that explicitly requires commercial or open-source C++ development. The candidate's C++ experience is dated (6 months at National Instruments in 2014-15) and recent work has pivoted to TypeScript/Node.js rather than systems programming. Without current C++ or Rust experience, onboarding to a production routing pipeline would require substantial ramp-up on the primary technology stack.
strengths: Direct experience building and maintaining geospatial data platforms (GISCollective, Green Map) with proven ability to work at scale
Demonstrated commitment to testing and quality assurance with TDD expertise and creation of testing frameworks (Fluent-Asserts, Trial)
Proven adaptability across multiple programming languages and willingness to expand expertise across full technical stacks
concerns: Critical gap in modern C++ proficiency—last significant C++ work was in 2014-15 at National Instruments, insufficient for a role where C++ is the primary requirement
No Rust experience despite it being explicitly listed as an acceptable alternative to C++
Recent career trajectory focused on TypeScript/Node.js and web application development, diverging from systems and low-level programming required for routing pipelines
